codeReview.summary: The implementation correctly adds conditional layout rendering infrastructure to BrainstormPage. When brainstormType is 'creative_spark', distinct CreativeSparkDesktopLayout/MobileLayout components render with a placeholder gallery area, conversation panel, and simplified CreativeSparkControls (no tool toggles, no model selector, no AgentVisualizer, no WorkspacePanel). When brainstormType is 'open_studio' or null, the existing BrainstormDesktopLayout/MobileLayout render unchanged. UI isolation is achieved cleanly — Creative Spark layouts import none of the Open Studio-specific components. The barrel export file is properly updated, and the conditional routing in BrainstormPage is straightforward and correct. All verification steps passed (lint, build, 183 backend tests).

issues.description: The component accepts BrainstormLayoutProps but only destructures ~11 of ~26+ props. The rest are silently ignored. This is acceptable for a placeholder that will be expanded in milestone 2, but could be confusing if someone expects the Creative Spark layout to actually use artifactList, selectedTools, etc. A comment or a narrower prop type would improve clarity, but not blocking since this is explicitly temporary infrastructure.

CreativeSparkControls defines its own CreativeSparkControlsProps type rather than deriving from or extending the existing BrainstormControls prop pattern. This is actually a good design choice for isolation — it means the Creative Spark controls have an independent contract. No issue, just noting the deliberate divergence.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The architecture.md describes Creative Spark layout as 'Full-screen masonry gallery + collapsible conversation panel + persistent controls', but the placeholder implementation uses a static centered placeholder with Sparkles icon rather than a collapsible conversation panel. The conversation panel in the placeholder is in a fixed right sidebar (desktop) or bottom section (mobile), not collapsible. This is expected for a placeholder but the architecture doc may mislead future workers into thinking the collapsible behavior already exists.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: architecture.md line 'Layout: Full-screen masonry gallery + collapsible conversation panel + persistent controls' vs CreativeSparkDesktopLayout.tsx which renders a fixed sidebar conversation panel without collapse controls

summary: Feature passes review. The conditional rendering infrastructure in BrainstormPage correctly routes between Open Studio and Creative Spark layouts based on brainstormType. All five expected behaviors are met: Open Studio renders all components (AgentVisualizer, WorkspacePanel, ConversationPanel, BrainstormControls with tool toggles and model selector), Creative Spark renders a distinct placeholder layout without any Open Studio elements, and UI isolation is cleanly enforced through separate component trees. The implementation is well-structured placeholder infrastructure ready for milestone 2 expansion. No blocking issues found.
